text.title: E2E-to-Contract Migration Recipe Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the E2E-to-Contract Migration Recipe methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Migration MUST start by extracting and committing the contract as a versioned schema file (OpenAPI, JSON Schema, Protobuf). 'Verbal contract' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a pinned contract, contract tests have nothing to assert against.

rule.statement: Migration ships BOTH provider-side and consumer-side contract tests. Migrating only one half re-creates the gap e2e was filling.
rule.rationale: Pact / contract-test patterns require both sides for full coverage.

rule.statement: Multi-step user flows (login → buy → receipt) STAY as e2e; only request/response surfaces move to contract.
rule.rationale: Contract tests cannot replace flow-shape coverage.

rule.statement: After migration, the schema file is the single source of truth; provider code, client SDKs, and docs all generate from it.
rule.rationale: multiple sources of truth always drift.

rule.statement: Migration spec records pre-migration flake rate + duration; post-migration spec compares.
rule.rationale: Without measurement, the migration's value is anecdote.

rule.statement: Spec declares how to roll back if contract tests miss a regression e2e would have caught (re-add the e2e, increase contract examples).
rule.rationale: Migrations without rollback paths become un-revisited monuments.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running E2E-to-Contract Migration Recipe under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
